criteria: The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand the overall NDAD methodology: hallucination signal generation, dynamic weighting, and gradient-descent-based logit adjustment.
The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand how hallucination signals are generated by masking critical attention heads based on head importance and layer-level entropy.
The agent has read Algorithm 1 to understand the full procedure for hallucination signal induction, including entropy computation, layer selection, head masking, and extraction of masked logits.

The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the dual-weight framework: global consistency measuring cosine similarity between masked and original logits, and local divergence measuring evolution trajectory alignment.

The agent has read Section 3.3 and Algorithm 2 completely to understand the full NDAD decoding pipeline: intra-layer normalization, inter-layer aggregation, KL divergence gradient computation, and final logit adjustment.
The agent has read Section 4.1 to understand the experimental setup, including all benchmark datasets, model variants, baselines, evaluation metrics, and hyperparameter settings.

The agent has read Appendix A.1 to check the detailed parameter settings: alpha=2, I=10, search ranges for alpha in {0.01, 0.1, 1, 2, 5, 10}, I in {5, 10, 20, 50}, and masked heads/layers in the range [6, 13].
The agent has read Table 1 to check the evaluation results of different methods on Llama2-7B/13B (base and chat) over TruthfulQA (MC1, MC2, MC3), FACTOR (Wiki), StrategyQA, and GSM8K.
The agent has read Table 2 to check the evaluation results on open-ended generation tasks (TriviaQA, PopQA, NQ-Open) for Llama2-7B/13B (base and chat).
The agent has read Table 3 to check the evaluation results on varying LLMs (Qwen2.5-7B-instruct, Mistral-7B-instruct, Llama3-8B-instruct) across TruthfulQA, FACTOR, and GSM8K.
The agent has read Table 4 to check the evaluation results on the larger-scale Llama2-70B model on FACTOR and GSM8K.
The agent has read Figure 3 and Table 6 to check the ablation study results verifying the role of hallucination signal induction, global weights, and local weights.
The agent has read Figure 4 to check the hyperparameter sensitivity experiments varying the number of masked heads and masked layers on Llama2-7B-base.

The plan includes a module for hallucination signal generation by computing per-layer head importance entropy, selecting top-K low-entropy layers, and masking top-x important heads within those layers.
The plan includes computing the global weight as a cosine similarity between the hallucination signal logits and original layer logits, scaled to [0, 1] via a linear mapping.

The plan includes constructing the tail distribution by suppressing top-I tokens in the masked logit distribution, and computing local weights as the cosine similarity of the hallucination evolution trajectory and the direction toward correctness candidates.

The plan includes combining global and local weights with a squared transformation, performing intra-layer normalization, applying inter-layer aggregation to form P_NDAD, and adjusting final logits via a gradient-descent step on the KL divergence.
The plan includes loading and preprocessing all benchmark datasets: TruthfulQA (MC1/MC2/MC3), FACTOR (Wiki), StrategyQA, GSM8K, TriviaQA, PopQA, and NQ-Open.
The plan includes loading all model variants: Llama2-7B-base, Llama2-7B-chat, Llama2-13B-base, Llama2-13B-chat, Llama2-70B, Qwen2.5-7B-instruct, Mistral-7B-instruct, and Llama3-8B-instruct.
The plan includes implementing all baselines: Greedy Decoding, DoLA-Low, DoLA-High, AD, and SLED.
The plan includes implementing evaluation metrics: factual accuracy for TruthfulQA (MC1/MC2/MC3) and CoT tasks, and Exact Match (EM) for TriviaQA, PopQA, and NQ-Open.
The plan includes ablation experiments: comparing random head masking, random layer masking, removal of global weight, and removal of local weight against the full NDAD method.
The plan includes hyperparameter sensitivity experiments varying the number of masked heads (x) and masked layers (K) across a range of [6, 13] and reporting results on FACTOR and GSM8K.

The code for computing head importance scores per layer has been implemented, following the method of Wu et al. (2024), to produce per-layer score lists {s_{l,1}, ..., s_{l,n}} for all n attention heads.
The code for computing layer-level entropy has been implemented as Equation 2: normalizing head scores into a probability distribution and computing E_l = -sum_i p_{l,i} log p_{l,i}.
The code for selecting the top-K layers with lowest entropy and masking the top-x scoring attention heads within those layers has been implemented as described in Algorithm 1.
The code for performing a forward pass through the LLM with selected attention heads zeroed out to extract per-layer masked logits {logits_1^mask, ..., logits_L^mask} has been implemented.
The code for computing the global weight W_l^global has been implemented as Equation 3: cosine similarity between logits_l and logits_l^mask, scaled to [0, 1] via a linear mapping phi.

The code for identifying the top-I tokens from the final-layer distribution P_logits_L and constructing I one-hot vectors {P_{e_1}, ..., P_{e_I}} as correctness direction candidates has been implemented.
The code for constructing the tail distribution P_logits_l^mask^tail by assigning a near-zero probability epsilon to the top-I tokens in P_logits_l^mask has been implemented.
The code for computing the local weight W_{l,i}^local has been implemented as Equation 5: max(cosine_sim(logits_l^mask - logits_L, P_logits_l^mask^tail - P_{e_i}), 0) for each i in [1, I].
The code for computing the combined weight W_{l,i} = W_l^global * W_{l,i}^local and applying the squared transformation W~_{l,i} = (W_{l,i})^2 has been implemented as Equations 6 and 7.
The code for intra-layer normalization of the squared weights has been implemented as Equation 8: P_l = (W~_{l,1}, ..., W~_{l,|I|}) / Z_l where Z_l = sum_{i=1}^{I} W~_{l,i}.
The code for inter-layer aggregation has been implemented as Equation 9: P_NDAD = sum_l N_l * P_l, where N_l = Z_l / sum_l Z_l, to obtain the final latent hallucination distribution.
The code for the final logit adjustment has been implemented as Equation 10: logits_L^new = logits_L - alpha * nabla KL(P_NDAD, P_logits_L), using a gradient-descent step on the KL divergence.

The complete NDAD decoding procedure has been implemented end-to-end as described in Algorithm 2, integrating hallucination signal induction, weight computation, P_NDAD aggregation, and logit adjustment.
The hyperparameters alpha=2 and I=10 have been set as default values, with search grids alpha in {0.01, 0.1, 1, 2, 5, 10} and I in {5, 10, 20, 50} implemented for tuning.
The number of masked heads (x) and masked layers (K) have been configured with a default range of [6, 13] in the hallucination signal induction module.
The code for loading and evaluating on the TruthfulQA dataset (MC1, MC2, MC3 metrics) has been implemented.
The code for loading and evaluating on the FACTOR (Wiki) dataset using factual accuracy has been implemented.
The code for loading and evaluating on StrategyQA and GSM8K chain-of-thought reasoning datasets has been implemented.
The code for loading and evaluating on open-ended generation datasets TriviaQA, PopQA, and NQ-Open using Exact Match (EM) metric has been implemented.
The DoLA-Low baseline (subtracting the most distributionally different layer from the first half of the network from final-layer logits) has been implemented.
The DoLA-High baseline (subtracting the most distributionally different layer from the second half of the network from final-layer logits) has been implemented.
The AD baseline (entropy-based contextual activation sharpness for decoding constraint) has been implemented.
The SLED baseline (integrating multiple early layers via weighted combination with gradient-descent adjustment) has been implemented.
The ablation variants have been implemented: (1) random head masking, (2) random layer masking, (3) NDAD without global weight, and (4) NDAD without local weight.
The code for measuring runtime (seconds) and memory usage (MB) of NDAD compared to Greedy, DoLA, and SLED on a single GSM8K sample with Llama2-7B-base has been implemented.

The code for main experiments comparing NDAD against all baselines on TruthfulQA (MC1/MC2/MC3), FACTOR, StrategyQA, and GSM8K using Llama2-7B/13B (base and chat) has been executed successfully without errors.
The code for open-ended generation experiments comparing NDAD against all baselines on TriviaQA, PopQA, and NQ-Open using Llama2-7B/13B (base and chat) has been executed successfully without errors.

The code for cross-model experiments evaluating NDAD on Qwen2.5-7B-instruct, Mistral-7B-instruct, and Llama3-8B-instruct across TruthfulQA, FACTOR, and GSM8K has been executed successfully without errors.
The code for large-scale model experiments evaluating NDAD on Llama2-70B over FACTOR and GSM8K has been executed successfully without errors.
The ablation experiments (random head, random layer, w/o global weight, w/o local weight) on Llama2-7B-base and Llama2-13B-base have been executed successfully without errors.

The hallucination signal verification experiment (comparing original decoding vs. masked-head decoding) on Llama2-7B/13B across Factor, StrQA, GSM8K, and TriviaQA has been executed successfully without errors.
The hyperparameter sensitivity experiments varying the number of masked heads and masked layers on Llama2-7B-base over FACTOR and GSM8K have been executed successfully without errors.
The runtime and memory overhead measurements for NDAD, DoLA, SLED, and Greedy on Llama2-7B-base have been executed successfully without errors.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: NDAD achieves higher or competitive average TruthfulQA accuracy (MC1, MC2, MC3 Avg.) compared to SLED across all four Llama2 model variants.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: NDAD outperforms all baselines on FACTOR (Wiki) for Llama2-7B-base (67.30), Llama2-13B-base (71.18), Llama2-7B-chat (65.06), and Llama2-13B-chat (67.74).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: NDAD consistently achieves the best GSM8K performance among all methods across all four Llama2 model variants (e.g., 14.86 for 7B-base, 29.26 for 13B-base).
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: the AD method shows near-zero StrategyQA and GSM8K performance on multiple Llama2 models, demonstrating its failure on CoT tasks.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: NDAD outperforms SLED on open-ended tasks, achieving higher TriviaQA, PopQA, and NQ-Open EM scores on Llama2-7B/13B variants.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3: NDAD achieves state-of-the-art performance on Qwen2.5-7B-instruct, Mistral-7B-instruct, and Llama3-8B-instruct across TruthfulQA (Avg.), FACTOR, and GSM8K.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 4: NDAD achieves 77.52 on FACTOR and 57.54 on GSM8K with Llama2-70B, outperforming SLED (77.32 and 57.01 respectively).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 5: NDAD has a runtime of approximately 1.34s and memory usage of approximately 17779 MB on Llama2-7B-base, higher than Greedy (1.11s, 13503 MB) and SLED (1.17s, 15452 MB).

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 3: masking important attention heads consistently degrades performance across all Llama2 models and datasets, with the most significant drop on GSM8K, confirming effective hallucination signal injection.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 6 (Llama2-7B-base): removing global weight or local weight from NDAD leads to performance drops, particularly on GSM8K, confirming the contribution of both weighting components.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 6: the full NDAD method (with both global and local weights and importance-guided masking) achieves the best or competitive performance compared to random head/layer masking variants.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 4: varying the number of masked heads and masked layers on Llama2-7B-base shows a general rising-then-falling trend on FACTOR and GSM8K, with optimal performance in the [6, 13] range.
